text.title: MCP Server Development Guide Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the mcp methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each MCP server MUST expose ≤ 20 tools. Servers exceeding the cap MUST be split into focused servers (e.g. github-issues, github-prs, github-actions).
rule.rationale: Past 20 tools, the server pollutes the model's tool namespace; tool selection accuracy drops sharply.

rule.statement: Every tool MUST declare its input schema (Zod for TS, Pydantic for Python). Schema-less tools are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without schema, the agent invents arguments; runtime validation cost exceeds the upfront schema cost.

rule.statement: Server MUST declare its transport: `stdio` for local installation, `streamable-http` for remote / multi-tenant. SSE transport is deprecated.
rule.rationale: Mixed transports produce flaky deploys; explicit choice forces upfront design.

rule.statement: Secrets MUST be passed via env vars at install time (`claude mcp add ... -e SECRET=...`). Hard-coded credentials in source are forbidden; CI MUST gitleaks-scan.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded credentials leak through public registries; env vars + gitleaks is the minimum bar.

rule.statement: Before publishing to npm / PyPI, MUST run `mcp-inspector` against the server and validate every tool's schema + sample invocation.
rule.rationale: Published servers without smoke test break for downstream users; inspector catches the common errors.
